Title: Site-directed genotype screening for elimination of antinutritional saponins in quinoa seeds identifies TSARL1 as a master controller of saponin biosynthesis selectively in seeds

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Down-regulated in tsarl1/sws mutants together with loss of seed saponin accumulation, suggesting contribution to saponin transport/sequestration in seeds
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Protein abundance of the ABC-type xenobiotic transporter AUR62041507 was significantly reduced in both sws1 and sws2 developing fruits. 2. It was among 22 proteins likely contributing to saponin biosynthesis/handling under TSARL1 control. 3. Its reduction correlates with abolished seed saponin accumulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Proteomics; mutant metabolite profiling; comparative analysis in tsarl1 loss-of-function lines
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in developing fruits/protein extracts; reduced in sws mutants
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly functionally validated by gene-specific knockout in this paper; included because the Results/Table 1 identify it as a likely contributor to saponin pathway output.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target for modulating seed saponin deposition or transport, but requires direct validation before deployment.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics; RNA-seq (related pathway survey, accession NA)

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Down-regulated in tsarl1/sws mutants together with seed saponin loss, suggesting contribution to saponin transport/sequestration in seeds
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AUR62042848 abundance was significantly decreased in sws1 and sws2 relative to WT. 2. It was classified among proteins likely contributing to saponin biosynthesis-related processes. 3. Its reduced abundance is associated with abolished seed saponin accumulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Proteomics; mutant metabolite profiling; comparative analysis in tsarl1 loss-of-function lines
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in developing fruits/protein extracts; reduced in sws mutants
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transport function inferred from annotation; direct transport assay not performed.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential secondary engineering target for altering seed saponin deposition after direct validation.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics; RNA-seq (related pathway survey, accession NA)

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms core triterpene scaffold and committed precursor for seed saponins
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as key control/committed-step enzyme in quinoa seed saponin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense compound; antinutritional factor in quinoa seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strong down-regulation in tsarl1 mutants is associated with near abolition of seed saponin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CqbAS1/LOC110726782 was strongly down-regulated at both transcript and protein levels in sws1 and sws2. 2. It was previously described to contribute to quinoa saponin biosynthesis and here is placed under TSARL1 control in seeds. 3. Reduced CqbAS1 abundance correlates with dramatic depletion of major seed saponins.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; proteomics; LC-qToF-MS/MS metabolite profiling in tsarl1 loss-of-function mutants; prior functional characterization cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in developing fruits; reduced in sws mutants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One additional BAS homolog (LOC110687932) was also strongly down-regulated, but CqbAS1 is the specifically highlighted characterized BAS gene.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppression expected to reduce seed saponins; possible defense-related consequences in seeds not established here.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful pathway target for lowering seed saponins, but upstream TSARL1 offers more selective seed-specific control.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms precursor scaffold for saponin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key committed-step enzyme family member; individual control status not fully resolved
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense compound; antinutritional factor in quinoa seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly down-regulated in tsarl1 mutants, consistent with reduced β-amyrin supply and loss of seed saponins
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A BAS homolog encoded by LOC110687932 was strongly down-regulated in both sws mutants. 2. It likely contributes to β-amyrin production for seed saponin biosynthesis. 3. Its reduced expression is associated with the low-saponin seed phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; comparative expression analysis in tsarl1 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing fruits; reduced in sws mutants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Less directly characterized than CqbAS1 in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary target for reducing saponins, but upstream TSARL1 is likely more practical.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes β-amyrin at C-28 to generate oleanolic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms committed oxidized aglycone precursor for quinoa seed saponins
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Highlighted as a major pathway control point under TSARL1 regulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense compound; antinutritional factor in quinoa seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly down-regulated at transcript and protein levels in tsarl1 mutants, associated with abolition of seed saponins
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP716A78 was strongly down-regulated in both sws1 and sws2 at transcript and protein levels. 2. It was previously characterized as a β-amyrin 28-oxidase synthesizing oleanolic acid from β-amyrin. 3. It showed one of the strongest protein decreases in sws mutants, supporting a major role in seed saponin biosynthesis under TSARL1 control.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; proteomics; LC-qToF-MS/MS metabolite profiling in tsarl1 mutants; prior enzymatic characterization cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing fruits; reduced in sws mutants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported as one of the enzymes catalyzing the committed step to oleanolic acid in quinoa.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppression likely reduces seed saponins; broader defense effects not resolved in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong pathway target, although TSARL1 provides more seed-selective upstream control.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes β-amyrin at C-28 to generate oleanolic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms committed oxidized aglycone precursor for quinoa seed saponins
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense compound; antinutritional factor in quinoa seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strong transcript down-regulation in tsarl1 mutants, associated with loss of seed saponins
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP716A79 transcripts were strongly down-regulated in both sws mutants. 2. The enzyme was previously characterized as a β-amyrin 28-oxidase that synthesizes oleanolic acid from β-amyrin. 3. Together with CYP716A78, it represents a committed step under TSARL1 regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; mutant metabolite profiling; prior enzymatic characterization cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing fruits; reduced in sws mutants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Protein-level detection not specifically highlighted in the table, but transcript-level evidence is clear.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH transcription factor with ACT-like C-terminal region
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense compound in plant tissues; antinutritional compound in quinoa seeds
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CqbAS1/LOC110726782; LOC110687932; CYP716A78/LOC110726788; CYP716A79/LOC110687936; broader saponin biosynthetic and mevalonate pathway genes; UGTs; ABC-type xenobiotic transporters
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates or positively controls expression of saponin biosynthetic genes in developing fruits/seeds
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of TSARL1 function abolishes or dramatically reduces seed-coat saponin accumulation while retaining saponins in leaves and roots
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master controller of seed saponin biosynthesis selectively in seeds
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS association mapping; EMS mutagenesis and site-directed genotype screening; homozygous loss-of-function mutants; RT-PCR transcript analysis; RNA-seq; proteomics; LC-qToF-MS/MS metabolite profiling; subcellular localization
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed only in reproductive tissues: fruits, flowers, fruit coats and seeds
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruits, flowers, fruit coats and seeds; not detected in roots, stems or leaves
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Downstream pathway genes/proteins inferred from regulatory control; direct protein partners not reported
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse and field-grown quinoa; long-day growth conditions used in many assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear penalty in tsarl1-3/sws1 for biomass, flowering, seed size, seed weight, or field yield; one tsarl1-4 backcrossed line showed reduced field performance under one trial, cause uncertain
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent breeding/editing target to eliminate antinutritional seed saponins while preserving leaf/root saponins and largely maintaining agronomic performance; allele selection or targeted knockout in commercial cultivars is promising
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter in mutant analysis; 35S promoter used only for heterologous TSARL1-eGFP localization in Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS mapped seed saponin content to a chromosome 16 region containing TSARL1, and TSARL1 haplotypes correlated with sweet versus bitter seeds. 2. Two independent loss-of-function tsarl1 alleles (sws1/tsarl1-3 and sws2/tsarl1-4) eliminated detectable seed-coat saponins, with >10-fold reduction in total abundance of identified seed saponins. 3. TSARL1 specifically regulates seed, not leaf/root, saponin biosynthesis. 4. TSARL1 positively controls expression of BAS and β-amyrin 28-oxidase genes and many additional saponin-pathway genes. 5. TSARL1 is expressed in reproductive tissues and localizes to the nucleus. 6. Seed quality improved without major growth or yield penalty in the main mutant line.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper distinguishes TSARL1 from TSARL2; TSARL2 showed only a conservative missense SNP not associated with sweet/bitter status. tsarl1-3 causes alternative splicing/truncation; tsarl1-4 introduces W4 stop codon. The authors conclude TSARL1 controls the committed step after 2,3-oxidosqualene selectively in seeds.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing/GWAS; RNA-seq; LC-qToF-MS/MS metabolomics; proteomics
